summary: This brief post announces two items: a new Bloomberg View column about false negatives in algorithmic decision-making for life-and-death situations like autonomous weapons, and an upcoming Occupy Alt Banking book club meeting. The Bloomberg piece argues that algorithms will perpetuate human mistakes unless we understand what data we're missing. The book club will discuss contrasting perspectives on capitalism through Milton Friedman's 'Capitalism and Freedom' and Eric Williams' 'Capitalism and Slavery.'
key_points: False negatives in algorithms can have lethal consequences, particularly in contexts like autonomous weapons
Algorithms inherit and repeat human blind spots unless we actively identify what's missing from our data
The juxtaposition of Friedman and Williams suggests an interest in examining capitalism from radically different ideological and historical perspectives
money_quotes: False Negatives Can Be a Matter of Life and Death
Algorithms will repeat our mistakes unless we know what we're missing
key_insight: Algorithmic systems amplify our existing blind spots, making the identification of false negatives—what we fail to see or measure—a matter of life and death.
